Package algatorgraph

Class GraphConverter

  • java.lang.Object
    • algatorgraph.GraphConverter
  • public class GraphConverter
extends java.lang.Object
    Utility class for converting graphs from JGraphT representations to custom graph implementations.

    Provides static methods to convert JGraphT graphs to custom graph implementations with generic vertex and weight types.

    Note: This converter assumes:

    • Edge weights are numeric types (Double, Integer, Float, etc.) This is because we assume the use of DefaultEdge and DefaultWeightedEdge
    • Vertex types are preserved during conversion
    • Graph directionality is preserved (directed JGraphT graphs -> DGraph, undirected -> Graph)

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      GraphConverter()

    • Method Detail

      • JGraphTToD

        public static <V,​W extends java.lang.Number> DGraph<V,​W> JGraphTToD​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph,
                                                                                java.lang.Class<W> weightClass)
        Converts a JGraphT graph (directed or undirected) to a DGraph with generic weight type.

        Important: This method always creates a DGraph regardless of whether the input graph is directed or undirected in JGraphT. For undirected graphs, consider using JGraphTToU(org.jgrapht.Graph, java.lang.Class) instead. 

         Usage example 1: Convert a directed JGraphT graph with Double weights
 Graph<Integer, DefaultWeightedEdge> jGraph = new DefaultDirectedWeightedGraph<>(DefaultWeightedEdge.class);
 jGraph.addVertex(1);
 jGraph.addVertex(2);
 DefaultWeightedEdge e = jGraph.addEdge(1, 2);
 jGraph.setEdgeWeight(e, 3.5);
 DGraph<Integer, Double> dGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToD(jGraph, Double.class);
 System.out.println(dGraph);
 
         Usage example 2: Convert imported graph with Integer weights
 List<Graph> graphs = GraphCreator.importGraphsFromFolder("path_to_folder");
 DGraph<String, Integer> dGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToD(graphs.get(0), Integer.class);
 System.out.println(dGraph);
 
         Usage example 3: Convert generated graph with Float weights
 String[] args = {"DIRECTED_SCALE_FREE", "0.3", "0.5", "0.1", "0.1", "50", "20"};
 Graph<Integer, DefaultEdge> jGraph = GraphCreator.generateGraph(args);
 DGraph<Integer, Float> dGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToD(jGraph, Float.class);
 System.out.println(dGraph);
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices in both input and output graphs
        W - the type of edge weights in the output graph (must extend Number)
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to convert (can be directed or undirected)
        weightClass - the class object representing the weight type (e.g., Double.class, Integer.class)
        Returns:
        a DGraph containing all vertices and edges from the input graph. Edge weights will be:
        • null for unweighted edges (DefaultEdge)
        • The original weight converted to type W for weighted edges (DefaultWeightedEdge)
        Throws:
        java.lang.ClassCastException - if vertices cannot be cast to type V or weights cannot be converted to type W
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if jgraphtGraph or weightClass is null
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if weightClass is not a supported numeric type

      • JGraphTToD

        public static <V> DGraph<V,​java.lang.Double> JGraphTToD​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph)
        Converts a JGraphT graph to a DGraph with Double weights (convenience method).
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to convert
        Returns:
        a DGraph with Double weights
        See Also:
        JGraphTToD(org.jgrapht.Graph, java.lang.Class)

      • JGraphTToU

        public static <V,​W extends java.lang.Number> Graph<V,​W> JGraphTToU​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph,
                                                                               java.lang.Class<W> weightClass)
        Converts a JGraphT undirected graph to an undirected Graph with generic weight type.

        Important: This method is designed for undirected JGraphT graphs. For directed graphs, use JGraphTToD(org.jgrapht.Graph, java.lang.Class). 

         Usage example 1: Convert a simple undirected graph with Double weights
 Graph<Integer, DefaultWeightedEdge> jGraph = new SimpleWeightedGraph<>(DefaultWeightedEdge.class);
 jGraph.addVertex(1);
 jGraph.addVertex(2);
 DefaultWeightedEdge e = jGraph.addEdge(1, 2);
 jGraph.setEdgeWeight(e, 2.5);
 Graph<Integer, Double> uGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToU(jGraph, Double.class);
 System.out.println(uGraph);
 
         Usage example 2: Convert generated complete graph with Integer weights
 String[] args = {"COMPLETE", "5"};
 Graph<Integer, DefaultEdge> jGraph = GraphCreator.generateGraph(args);
 Graph<Integer, Integer> uGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToU(jGraph, Integer.class);
 System.out.println(uGraph);
 
         Usage example 3: Convert imported graph with custom weight type
 List<Graph> graphs = GraphCreator.importGraphsFromFolder("path_to_folder");
 Graph<String, Float> uGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToU(graphs.get(0), Float.class);
 System.out.println(uGraph);
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices in both input and output graphs
        W - the type of edge weights in the output graph (must extend Number)
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT undirected graph to convert
        weightClass - the class object representing the weight type (e.g., Double.class, Integer.class)
        Returns:
        a Graph (undirected) containing all vertices and edges from the input. Edge weights will be:
        • null for unweighted edges (DefaultEdge)
        • The original weight converted to type W for weighted edges (DefaultWeightedEdge)
        Throws:
        java.lang.ClassCastException - if vertices cannot be cast to type V or weights cannot be converted to type W
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if jgraphtGraph or weightClass is null
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if weightClass is not a supported numeric type

      • JGraphTToU

        public static <V> Graph<V,​java.lang.Double> JGraphTToU​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph)
        Converts a JGraphT undirected graph to a Graph with Double weights (convenience method).
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to convert
        Returns:
        a Graph with Double weights
        See Also:
        JGraphTToU(org.jgrapht.Graph, java.lang.Class)

      • isDirected

        public static boolean isDirected​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph)
        Determines whether a JGraphT graph should be converted to directed or undirected format.

        This helper method can be used before conversion to decide which converter to use. 

         Usage example:
 org.jgrapht.Graph jGraph = // ... get a graph
 if (GraphConverter.isDirected(jGraph)) {
     DGraph<Integer, Double> dGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToD(jGraph, Double.class);
     // Process as directed graph
 } else {
     Graph<Integer, Double> uGraph = GraphConverter.JGraphTToU(jGraph, Double.class);
     // Process as undirected graph
 }
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to check
        Returns:
        true if the graph is directed, false if undirected
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if jgraphtGraph is null

      • convert

        public static <V,​W extends java.lang.Number> java.lang.Object convert​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph,
                                                                            java.lang.Class<W> weightClass)
        Unified converter that automatically detects graph directionality and converts with the specified weight type. 
         Usage example:
 org.jgrapht.Graph jGraph = // ... get a graph
 Object convertedGraph = GraphConverter.convert(jGraph, Double.class);
 
 if (convertedGraph instanceof DGraph) {
     DGraph<Integer, Double> dGraph = (DGraph<Integer, Double>) convertedGraph;
     // Process as directed graph
 } else {
     Graph<Integer, Double> uGraph = (Graph<Integer, Double>) convertedGraph;
     // Process as undirected graph
 }
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices
        W - the type of edge weights
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to convert
        weightClass - the class object representing the weight type
        Returns:
        either a DGraph or Graph based on the input graph's directionality
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if jgraphtGraph or weightClass is null

      • convert

        public static <V> java.lang.Object convert​(org.jgrapht.Graph jgraphtGraph)
        Unified converter that automatically detects graph directionality and converts with Double as the default weight type. 
         Usage example:
 org.jgrapht.Graph jGraph = // ... get a graph
 Object convertedGraph = GraphConverter.convert(jGraph);
 
 if (convertedGraph instanceof DGraph) {
     DGraph<Integer, Double> dGraph = (DGraph<Integer, Double>) convertedGraph;
     // Process as directed graph
 } else {
     Graph<Integer, Double> uGraph = (Graph<Integer, Double>) convertedGraph;
     // Process as undirected graph
 }
        Type Parameters:
        V - the type of vertices
        Parameters:
        jgraphtGraph - the JGraphT graph to convert
        Returns:
        either a DGraph or Graph based on the input graph's directionality
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if jgraphtGraph is null
        See Also:
        convert(org.jgrapht.Graph, java.lang.Class)
